design_philosophy.core_loop: Fly freely in 2D space, rotate aim independently from movement, defeat enemies, collect Zenny currency, purchase weapon upgrades from shops between stages, progress through 9 stages to defeat Bios
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Decoupled aiming system creates risk-reward: maintain positioning while rotating aim in 16 directions; manage vitality gauge against screen-filling enemy fire while deciding when to use limited megacrush attacks
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Supersoldier with advanced aerial combat capabilities; autonomous satellite providing backup firepower; upgrading arsenal across progression to become increasingly powerful
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentional design choice to decouple movement and aiming—satellite orbits independently and can be repositioned mid-flight without affecting player character movement. This creates a unique skill expression compared to traditional shmups where aim follows movement. Capcom deliberately made the game easier than previous shooters after criticism about projectile dodging difficulty, signaling player-friendly difficulty philosophy despite arcade pedigree.

difficulty.lives_system: Single life; vitality gauge depletes with enemy contact/projectile hits
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present; player given chance to continue after vitality runs out
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; nine stages with boss at each stage conclusion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: purchase defensive items (health kits, armor) from shops to reduce incoming damage; buy tips from shops for boss strategies; weapon upgrades from Zenny economy directly affect combat capability; megacrush attack trades vitality for screen clear, creating risk-management decisions

progression.upgrade_system: Currency-based (Zenny coins) shop system between stages; upgradeable satellite weapons, health restoration, armor plating; tips purchasable for boss encounters
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression linear; boss defeats unlock next stage; shop items unlocked via Zenny collection
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon configurations available through shop
Dual-player cooperative mode (Player 1 long-range rifle vs Player 2 wide-shot short-range)
Satellite positioning micromanagement creates varied combat approaches
Continue system allows extended play sessions

world_building_techniques: Setting exposition: 29th century post-apocalyptic Dust World created by evil god Bios
Two nameless supersoldiers as player avatars (minimal characterization)
Eight evil gods serving Bios as stage-progression antagonists

boss_design.philosophy: Nine boss encounters (one per stage), each representing one of Bios's eight evil gods plus Bios himself. Boss encounter tips purchasable from shop system, indicating designed learning curve and pattern-based encounters rather than pure reflex tests.

faction_indicators: Two distinct player archetypes: Player 1 (long-range automatic rifle), Player 2 (short-range wide shot)
Cooperative multiplayer incentivizes coordinated satellite positioning strategies
danger_signals: Screen-filling enemy projectile patterns requiring constant repositioning
Vitality gauge depletion as failure state
Time-limited shop decisions requiring quick purchasing choices
Megacrush attack costs vitality—players must judge when screen clear justifies health loss
environmental_storytelling: Desolate wasteland setting (Dust World) communicated through visual design
Shop encounters as safe harbors within hostile stages
Progressive boss encounters as manifestation of Bios's nine-part evil hierarchy
